name: t5-mock-protocol-drift
isolation_opt_out_reason: Committed directly on main 2026-06-10 as part of a small test-coverage/index chore batch; no feature/* or chore/* branch existed. Accepted historically — BRANCH_ISOLATION_HISTORICAL advisory silenced 2026-06-17 (data-integrity sweep).
primary_metric: Central protocol-conformance registry: every test-mocked protocol has a compile-time anchor so a protocol-surface drift fails the build at a single labelled point.

success_metrics.primary: MockProtocolConformanceTests.swift anchors all 6 test-mocked protocols (AnalyticsProvider/GoogleAuthProviding/AppleAuthProviding/EmailAuthProviding/URLSessionProtocol/AIInputAdapter); test target compiles; build-for-testing green
secondary: 2 shareable app-target mocks (MockAnalyticsAdapter, MockGoogleAuthProvider) additionally bound + smoke-tested
Documented the scoping reframe: spec's named mocks don't exist + real mocks are private-by-design

kill_criteria: Anchors duplicate private mocks in a way that creates maintenance burden without catching new drift.
A protocol gains an optional/defaulted method that the anchor masks (false negative).
kill_criteria_resolution: Neither fired. The anchors are minimal (one per protocol) and centralized — they ADD a single labelled failure point that Swift's per-mock conformance doesn't provide (the real mocks are private to their test files). No protocol in scope uses optional/defaulted requirements, so an added requirement always breaks the anchor (no masking).

tasks.summary: Scope: spec's named mocks absent; real mocks mostly private; conformance already compile-enforced -> reframe to central registry

prd.note: b_medium; scope in test-coverage plan T5
